  • XSD primitives check
    properties:
    haxdoc: :group
    end:
#+endsection

Imports

xml_ast, hstring_algo, halgorithm, clformat, oswrap, helpers, colorstring

Types

XsdEntryKind = enum
  xekComplexComplexType,    ## Complex type with complex content
  xekComplexSimpleType,     ## Complex type with simple content
  xekComplexType, xekSimpleType, ## Simple type
  xekElement,               ## `xsd:element`
  xekAttribute,             ## `xsd:attribute`
  xekGroupDecl,             ## `xsd:group`
  xekChoice,                ## `xsd:choice`
  xekGroupRef,              ## `xsd:group ref=`
  xekSequence,              ## `xsd:sequence`. Ordered list of elements
  xekExtension,             ## `xsd:extension`
  xekRestriction,           ## `xsd:restriction`
  xekSimpleContent,         ## `xsd:simpleContent`
  xekTop,                   ## Toplevel element in schema document
  xekEnumeration,           ## `xsd:enumeration`
  xekPattern
XsdEntry = ref object
  xsdName*: Option[string]   ## `name=""` attribute
  xsdType: Option[string]    ## `type=""` attribute
  xsdMinOccurs*: Option[XsdOccursCount] ## `minOccurs`
  xsdMaxOccurs*: Option[XsdOccursCount] ## `maxOccurs`
  subnodes: seq[XsdEntry]
  attrs*: StringTableRef
  xsdTypeImpl*: Option[XsdEntry] ## Reference to the type implementation
  case kind*: XsdEntryKind
  of xekComplexSimpleType:
      cstContent: string

  of xekGroupRef:
      groupName*: string
      groupRef*: XsdEntry    ## Reference to group implementation
    
  of xekTop:
      startElement: XsdEntry

  of xekEnumeration:
      value*: string

  else:
      nil

  
XsdDocument = object
  entry*: XsdEntry

XsdParser = object
  case onKind*: bool
  of true:
      kind*: XsdTokenKind

  of false:
      tag*: string

  parser*: string
  entry*: Option[XsdEntry]

XsdParsers = object
  onKinds*: seq[XsdParser]
  onNames*: seq[XsdParser]

Consts

xekGroupDeclare = xekGroupDecl

Procs

proc add(xsd: var XsdEntry; subnode: XsdEntry) {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc `==`(xsd; kind: XsdEntryKind): bool {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc treeRepr(xsd; colored: bool = true; indexed: bool = false;
              maxdepth: int = 120): string {...}{.raises: [ValueError], tags: [].}
proc newTree(kind: XsdEntryKind; subnodes: varargs[XsdEntry]): XsdEntry {...}{.
    raises: [], tags: [].}
proc isEnumerationType(xsd): bool {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc isChoiceType(xsd): bool {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc isMixed(xsd): bool {...}{.raises: [KeyError], tags: [].}
proc isOptional(xsd): bool {...}{.raises: [KeyError], tags: [].}
Whether entry described by xsd has explicit use="optional" tag, or has minOccurs equal to zero 
proc isUnboundedRepeat(xsd): bool {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
Whether entry descibed by xsd does not have finite upper repeat bound. Lower repeat bound can be anything 
proc isFinite(xsd): bool {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc getAttributes(xsd): seq[XsdEntry] {...}{.raises: [UnexpectedKindError], tags: [].}
proc getElements(xsd): seq[XsdEntry] {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc isPrimitiveRestriction(xsd): bool {...}{.raises: [UnexpectedKindError], tags: [].}
Whether entry described by xsd is a restriction for some base type (xsd:string that matches particular patern for example) 
proc isPrimitiveExtension(xsd): bool {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
Whether entry described by xsd is a restriction for some base type (xsd:string that matches particular patern for example) 
proc isPrimitiveType(xsdType: string): bool {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc isAlwaysPrimitiveType(xsd): bool {...}{.raises: [KeyError], tags: [].}

Whether entry directly has a primitive type (or primitive type itself), or it's type is a restriction of some basic type.

For example xsd:string is a primitive, type, and so is DoxBool - because it is defined as an enumeration restriction based on primitive type.

<xsd:simpleType name="DoxBool">
  <xsd:restriction base="xsd:string">
    <xsd:enumeration value="yes" />
    <xsd:enumeration value="no" />
  </xsd:restriction>
</xsd:simpleType>

Any XSD entry that has type DoxBool will always be a primitive type.

proc isPossiblePrimitiveType(xsd): bool {...}{.
    raises: [KeyError, ImplementKindError], tags: [].}
Similar to isAlwaysPrimitiveType, but checks whether object might
/potentially/ be represented using primitive entry, or it always has an

tag.

proc classifyPrimitiveTypeKind(str: string): XsdTokenKind {...}{.
    raises: [ArgumentError], tags: [].}
proc classifyPrimitiveTypeKind(xsd): XsdTokenKind {...}{.
    raises: [ArgumentError, KeyError], tags: [].}
proc namePrimitiveTypeKind(kind: XsdTokenKind; withPrefix: bool = false): string {...}{.
    raises: [ArgumentError], tags: [].}
proc getNimName(kind: XsdTokenKind): string {...}{.raises: [UnexpectedKindError],
    tags: [].}
proc getParserName(kind: XsdTokenKind): string {...}{.raises: [ArgumentError],
    tags: [].}
proc getNimType(name: string): string {...}{.raises: [UnexpectedKindError,
    ArgumentError], tags: [].}
proc getFirstTokens(xsd; parent: XsdEntry = nil): seq[
    tuple[token: XsdToken, source: Option[XsdEntry]]] {...}{.
    raises: [KeyError, ArgumentError, UnexpectedKindError], tags: [].}
Return list of tokens that entry described by xsd can start with. 
proc getFirstSet(xsd): set[XsdTokenKind] {...}{.
    raises: [KeyError, ArgumentError, UnexpectedKindError], tags: [].}
proc getExpectedKinds(parsers: XsdParsers): set[XsdTokenKind] {...}{.raises: [],
    tags: [].}
proc getFirstParsers(alts: seq[XsdEntry]): XsdParsers {...}{.
    raises: [KeyError, ArgumentError, UnexpectedKindError], tags: [].}

Return token kind -> parser name mapping for each element in alts.

#[ - @arg{alts} :: FIRST set of tokens for arbitrary entry. Use ]# #[ [[code:/getFirstTokens()]] to generate this set. ]# # Argument error raises

  • @raise{ArgumentError} :: if alts have overlapping FIRST sets
  • @ret{onTokenKinds} :: Starting token kind mapped to parser name. element start/ends are not added in this list (because element parsing is also based on <tagname, and so might have mutiple different mapping for single token). Primitive parser names are used as defined in [[code:xml_ast]] module. # NOTE need to make sure [[code:xml_ast]] is # resolved correctly into module name. I should look into json pointer # implementation and base parser on that.
  • @ret{onTokenNames} :: Mapping from <tagname to parser name (based on type). Parser names are constructed using "parse" + alt.getType()
proc getExtensionSection(xsd): XsdEntry {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc xsd(str: string): string {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc updateBaseAttrs(xsd: var XsdEntry; node: XmlNode) {...}{.raises: [ValueError],
    tags: [].}
proc convertElement(xml): XsdEntry {...}{.raises: [ValueError], tags: [].}
proc convertAttribute(xml; context): XsdEntry {...}{.raises: [ValueError], tags: [].}
proc convertGroup(xml; context): XsdEntry {...}{.
    raises: [Exception, ImplementError, KeyError, ValueError],
    tags: [RootEffect].}
proc convertSequence(xml; context): XsdEntry {...}{.
    raises: [ImplementError, ValueError, Exception, KeyError],
    tags: [RootEffect].}
proc convertExtension(xml; context): XsdEntry {...}{.
    raises: [ValueError, ImplementError], tags: [].}
proc convertRestriction(xml, context): XsdEntry {...}{.
    raises: [ValueError, ImplementError], tags: [].}
proc convertComplexType(xml; context): XsdEntry {...}{.
    raises: [ValueError, ImplementError, Exception, KeyError],
    tags: [RootEffect].}
proc convertSimpleType(xml, context): XsdEntry {...}{.
    raises: [ValueError, ImplementError], tags: [].}
proc convertSchema(xml): XsdEntry {...}{.raises: [ValueError, ImplementError,
    Exception, KeyError], tags: [RootEffect].}
proc parseXsd(file: AbsFile): XsdEntry {...}{.raises: [ValueError, ImplementError,
    Exception, KeyError, IOError, OSError, XmlError],
    tags: [RootEffect, ReadIOEffect, WriteIOEffect].}
proc parseXsd(text: string): XsdEntry {...}{.raises: [ValueError, ImplementError,
    Exception, KeyError, IOError, OSError, XmlError], tags: [RootEffect,
    ReadIOEffect, WriteIOEffect].}

Funcs

func `[]`(xsd; idx: int | BackwardsIndex): XsdEntry
func len(xsd): int {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
func hasName(xsd): bool {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
func name(xsd): string {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
func hasType(xsd): bool {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
func hasAttr(xsd; attr: string): bool {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
func hasAttr(xsd; key, value: string): bool {...}{.raises: [KeyError], tags: [].}
func `[]`(xsd; key: string): string {...}{.raises: [KeyError], tags: [].}
func getType(xsd): string {...}{.raises: [KeyError], tags: [].}
Return type name for xsd - either type="" attribute, or name of the type itself (if xsd is a simple/complex type) 
func hasTypeImpl(xsd): bool {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
func getTypeImpl(xsd): XsdEntry {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
func subnodes(xsd): seq[XsdEntry] {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
func `$`(occur: XsdOccursCount): string {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}

iterator pairs(xsd): (int, XsdEntry) {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
